metadata.description: Binance Staked ETH (wBETH) is a value-accruing liquid staking receipt token issued by Binance, currently sized at $8.18B circulating. Users supply ETH to the wBETH contract and receive wBETH 1:1; the redemption rate then drifts upward as Binance applies an off-chain `updateExchangeRate` from a single dedicated EOA. The protocol has no on-chain governance: every admin role (proxy admin, owner/pauser/blacklister/masterMinter, operator, rescuer, recharge address) is held by a Binance-controlled EOA, and the actual mint authority sits in a separate Binance-deployed `OperatorWallet` proxy that is itself controlled by another set of Binance EOAs. wBETH has no external dependencies (no oracle, no other protocol) — the exchange rate is set by Binance internally and pushed on-chain.

admins.name: wBETH/Unwrap Proxy Admin (EOA)
admins.description: Single Binance EOA that is the proxy admin (`$admin`) of both the wBETH wrap proxy and the Unwrap proxy. Can call `upgradeTo`, `upgradeToAndCall`, and `changeAdmin` on either proxy at will, replacing the implementation logic of the protocol's two user-facing contracts without delay or governance review.

admins.name: wBETH Protocol Owner (EOA)
admins.description: Single Binance EOA that simultaneously holds `owner`, `pauser`, `blacklister`, and `masterMinter` on WrapTokenV3ETH and `owner`/`pauser`/`blacklister` on UnwrapTokenV1ETH, plus `owner` on ExchangeRateUpdater. This one key can pause and unpause, blacklist or unblacklist any user, configure or remove minters, configure or remove rate-update callers, and rotate every other admin role on every contract.

admins.name: OperatorWallet (Minter)
admins.description: Binance-deployed upgradeable contract that is the sole entry in wBETH's `minters` set, authorised to call `mint(uint256)` and `burn(uint256)` on wBETH. Its own admin and owner are a separate pair of Binance EOAs (see below); the result is a two-hop minting trust chain that ultimately resolves to single-key control.

admins.name: Zero Address (Renounced)
admins.description: The `rescuer` role on wBETH, which would otherwise let its holder sweep stuck ERC-20s out of the contract via `rescueERC20`, is set to the zero address. This permission is effectively renounced — no party can invoke `rescueERC20` until the owner reassigns the role.

admins.name: ExchangeRateUpdater
admins.description: Internal protocol contract registered as the `oracle` on wBETH; its only privilege is calling `updateExchangeRate` on wBETH. The contract itself is owner-gated via the wBETH Protocol Owner EOA above and rate-limited per authorised caller. Listed as an admin only because it sits between the operator EOA and the wBETH state, not because it is a human controller.

admins.name: Protocol Operator (EOA)
admins.description: Single Binance EOA registered as `operator` on both wBETH and the Unwrap contract, and as the only authorised entry in ExchangeRateUpdater's `callers` set. Controls all daily fund movements (`supplyEth`, `moveToStakingAddress`, `moveToUnwrapAddress`, `moveToBackAddress`, `allocate`), the `lockTime`/`ethStaked` operational parameters on Unwrap, and the rate-limited `updateExchangeRate` call that adjusts wBETH's redemption ratio against ETH.

admins.name: Unwrap Recharge Address (EOA)
admins.description: Single Binance EOA registered as `rechargeAddress` on UnwrapTokenV1ETH. Its only on-chain authority is calling `rechargeFromRechargeAddress` to top up the contract's ETH float so it can satisfy queued unwrap claims.

admins.name: WrapTokenV3ETH (Self)
admins.description: The wBETH contract itself is the only authorised caller of `moveFromWrapContract` on UnwrapTokenV1ETH. This is a contract-to-contract call gate (the `onlyWrapTokenAddress` modifier), not a human admin — it cannot be invoked from outside the wBETH wrap flow.

admins.name: OperatorWallet Proxy Admin (EOA)
admins.description: Single Binance EOA that is the `$admin` of the OperatorWallet proxy. Can replace the OperatorWallet implementation at any time via `upgradeTo`/`upgradeToAndCall`. A successful upgrade gives the new implementation full control of the wBETH minter (since OperatorWallet is the sole entry in `minters`).

admins.name: OperatorWallet Owner (EOA)
admins.description: Single Binance EOA that owns the OperatorWallet contract. Can call `mint` and `transfer` (the wBETH-minting and ERC-20-transfer entry points on OperatorWallet), `addHotWallet`/`removeHotWallet`, `changeOperator`, `pause`/`unpause`, `withdraw`, and the unrestricted `execTransaction(target, value, data)` — an arbitrary-call escape hatch with no scope or amount limit.

admins.name: OperatorWallet Operator (EOA)
admins.description: Single Binance EOA registered as `operator` on the OperatorWallet contract. Authorised to call `mint(token, recipient, amount)` and `transfer(token, recipient, amount)` on OperatorWallet — i.e. can independently mint wBETH (within the `notHotWallet` recipient guard) without the OperatorWallet Owner's signature.

funds.name: WrapTokenV3ETH (wBETH)
funds.description: The wBETH token contract itself. Circulating supply is $8.18B, representing every staked ETH and accrued staking rewards held by Binance on users' behalf since the 2023-04-27 launch. The contract also keeps a small operational ETH buffer of $2.17M on-chain (used to satisfy instant unwraps before they hit the queued Unwrap flow); the rest of the staked ETH sits in Binance's off-chain validator infrastructure.

governance.proposalRequirements: No on-chain proposal mechanism. All admin actions are Binance corporate decisions, signed by Binance-held EOAs that hold owner, pauser, blacklister, masterMinter, operator, rescuer, and proxy-admin roles on the wBETH/Unwrap/ExchangeRateUpdater/OperatorWallet contracts.
governance.votingProcess: No on-chain voting. Binance executes via single-key EOAs holding owner, pauser, masterMinter, operator, and proxy admin roles.
